title: Halloumi & Roasted Grape Giant Couscous
summary: Roasted grapes are a current obsession - we love how jammy they go when they burst. Their sweetness is perfect with salty halloumi in this simple, one pan veggie tray bake that you’ll be making on repeat.

ingredients.label: ½ Tsp Smoked Paprika, Plus Extra to Serve
ingredients.name: Smoked Paprika

ingredients.label: Handful of Nuts of Your Choice (We Like Flaked Almonds, Hazelnuts or Walnuts)
ingredients.name: Mixed Nuts

method.description: Rinse the giant couscous in a sieve then tip into a medium roasting tin. Finely slice the red onion, add to the tin along with the grapes on their vine. Mix the smoked paprika with the vegetable stock then pour this into the tin. Give everything a good stir.
Cover in tin foil and roast for 25 mins. Meanwhile toast the nuts of your choice, roughly chop if needed. Slice the halloumi.
Remove the tin from the oven - the couscous should now be cooked, take off the tinfoil and stir through the kale.
Turn the grill to high. Lay the halloumi slices on top of the traybake, drizzle over the honey then slide under the grill until the halloumi is nicely golden and grapes bursting, 3-5 mins.
Scatter over the toasted nuts and dill, plus a sprinkle more smoked paprika, to serve.

chefs.name: Sophie Godwin
chefs.summary: Sophie Godwin is a cookbook author, food writer and chef. When she’s not catering weddings, she is rustling up accessible cookable bangers. She's helped us write multiple Mob cookbooks over the years and her recipes never disappoint. Her debut cookbook Sundays is out now.
